idea: ROADMAP entries will change significantly post-standardization
Grand Plan debt items may become invalid as files change
State persistence should use whatever resources are best
Future idea capture mechanism needed
Cross-ecosystem subsystem mapping is its own discovery task
Canon is Ecosystem Zero — all others derive from it
Planning standardization (deep-plan, GSD) as backbone — build from there
PR creep guardrail: commit counter hook (warn 10, block 25, override available)
Branch scope declaration idea: S/M/L with commit brackets
Session-end PR gate: flag if >15 commits not in a PR
State saving rules need to be canonized
13 cross-cutting subsystems
Configuration as single source of truth vs hardcoded values
Per-ecosystem required-vs-optional subsystem mapping needed
L5 is north star not exit criteria — most target L3-L4 baseline
Upgrade-friendliness as design principle in every component
MCP memory as secondary state backup (verify next session)
Configure episodic memory for remote sessions (broader benefit beyond this plan)
Interactivity-first as a design tenet for all skills and tooling — batch questioning > monologue output
Health report dual format (JSON envelope + JSONL findings) could become a general pattern for all ecosystem outputs
Core tenets discovery phase should precede schema/structure decisions — tenets guide everything downstream
Tenets should be CANON's first artifact (.canon/tenets.jsonl) — CANON defines itself before it defines others
CROSS_PLATFORM_SETUP.md is stale — references removed sync script. Needs update when platform-agnostic tenet is implemented
Cascade/override (D#5) and progressive disclosure are useful patterns but not tenets — keep as referenced patterns, not principles
17 tenets across 4 categories is the right granularity — specific enough to be actionable, broad enough to be stable

Roadmap & Execution ecosystem is the HUB — all other ecosystems feed work items into it. Non-debt pipelines (audit findings → roadmap, deep-plan decisions → roadmap, feature planning → roadmap) are completely missing. T8 violation.
Roadmap & Execution needs high implementation priority — if we standardize 13 ecosystems, each generates work items needing automated intake
CANON enforcement cascade: version broadcast → health checker detection → migration automation → fail-loud escalation → staggered rollout. Both self-protection and downstream propagation.
deep-plan decisions should auto-generate ROADMAP sprint items — manual copying is T8 violation
audit-aggregator produces report but nobody places findings into sprints unless human does it — another missing pipeline
App-layer ecosystem planning is fundamentally different from process ecosystem planning — connection points between layers are the priority, not internal structure
Operational sprint health monitoring items may overlap with ecosystem standardization health monitoring — need reconciliation during sequencing
Planning docs (ROADMAP, sprints, grand plan, future roadmap) need continuous updates during plan execution — additions AND deletions of completed/deprecated items
Audits L5 pathway should be architecturally designed during L4 work — versioned audit standards, automated regression detection, self-auditing capabilities
Checklist adaptation formalization: rigid framework + earned flexibility. Per-ecosystem mapping with required justification for any deviation from the 16-item standard
Wave structure allows both parallelism (within waves) and learning transfer (between waves) — hybrid approach
TDMS as cross-wave staged effort — each wave advances it one level
Skills elevation to Wave 2 creates a heavy wave — may need 2A/2B split or careful parallel tracks
Track-CANON in ROADMAP.md as the overhaul's big-picture sequence, with individual items in natural tracks
ROADMAP additions should trigger dedupe against MASTER_DEBT to prevent duplicate entries
Knowledge base concept: all prior research (framework repo, PR Review v2, research agents, decision logs, standards) form a reusable database for every ecosystem deep-plan Phase 0
3+ sessions without standardization progress should trigger ecosystem health dashboard flag (T8 automation over discipline)
Grand Plan pre-checks before each ecosystem: scan changelog for MASTER_DEBT items referencing files about to be modified
Changelog extensible-core schema mirrors D22 ecosystem registry pattern — required fields + optional context
CANON version trajectory: 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (checkpoint #4) → 0.3.0 (checkpoint #7) → 0.4.0 (checkpoint #15) → 1.0.0 (checkpoint #21)
